title: Interface declared in parent scope can be used incorrectly

features.name: interface declared at top-level parent scope
features.description: Code should include an interface declared outside any module at the top-level (parent) scope, not nested within a module

features.name: upward name referencing of interface instance from sibling module
features.description: Code should include a module inside another module that accesses an interface instance declared in its parent module's sibling scope, using upward name referencing

features.name: interface port access from child module
features.description: Code should include a child module that accesses an interface instance belonging to its enclosing module's sibling through hierarchical path reference
